notes: Python 1.5.19 — JANGTQ-PRESTACK STANDARD support + MiniMax EOS fix + DSML routing through encoding_dsv4. (1) JANGTQ-PRESTACK STANDARD: bundles that ship routed expert tensors pre-stacked under `{prefix}.{ffn|mlp|block_sparse_moe}.switch_mlp.{gate_proj|up_proj|down_proj|gate_up_proj}.{tq_packed|tq_norms|tq_bits}` with shapes [n_experts, out, packed_in] are loaded as-is — no sidecar fast-load, no streaming restack. Verified end-to-end on DeepSeek-V4-VMLX (256 experts) and MiniMax-M2.7-JANGTQ (62 layers × 3 experts). DSV4 sidecar relocated to `~/.cache/vmlx-engine/jangtq-stacked/` with bundle-dir back-compat read. (2) MiniMax EOS — eos_token_id=200020 only present in generation_config.json (not config.json) was producing endless `[e~[` token loops; loader now merges generation_config eos_ids. (3) DSML tool-call extraction — DeepSeek-V4 outputs go through `encoding_dsv4.parse_message_from_completion_text` first, with regex fallback. (4) Ling-2.6-flash (`bailing_hybrid`) runtime support — vendored bailing_hybrid.py, fixed MTP cache slot crash, P15 gate-skip guard, think_in_template=False (Ling defaults thinking off). Inherits 1.5.16/1.5.17/1.5.18: bundled mlx_lm fp32 SDPA absorb branch (deepseek_v3/v32/mistral4); JANG_4K allocator routed-expert protection; paged + disk L2 cache (1.74× warm-prefix); Ollama/Anthropic/Responses gateway protocol fan-out; VMLX_MODELS_DIR. Apple notarized + stapled. SHA-256: c005fb1fcbff563436965f5e04ad1f99d6644c3d70b7b80d44da20511072cdb3
